# Internationalization (i18n) Reference

Use this reference when building UI that may be translated, localized, or used across locales.
i18n is not just translation — it includes formatting, layout direction, cultural conventions, and content flexibility.

---

## Core Rules

1. Never hardcode user-facing strings in components. Use constants, i18n keys, or CMS content.
2. Never build dates, numbers, or currencies with string concatenation. Use `Intl` formatters.
3. Never embed text in images. Use HTML text with CSS styling.
4. Always design layouts that can accommodate ~30–40% longer text (German, Finnish, etc.).
5. Always set the `lang` attribute on `<html>` or per-section where language changes.

---

## String Externalization

### Don't Hardcode Strings

```tsx
// Wrong — hardcoded strings are invisible to translators
<button>Save Changes</button>
<p>No results found. Try a different search.</p>
<label>Email Address</label>

// Correct — externalized strings
<button>{t("actions.save")}</button>
<p>{t("search.empty")}</p>
<label>{t("form.email_label")}</label>
```

### String Keys Convention

```ts
// Flat structure — simple, good for small apps
{
  "save": "Save Changes",
  "cancel": "Cancel",
  "search_empty": "No results found"
}

// Nested structure — organized, good for large apps
{
  "actions": {
    "save": "Save Changes",
    "cancel": "Cancel",
    "delete": "Delete"
  },
  "search": {
    "placeholder": "Search...",
    "empty": "No results found. Try a different search.",
    "results_count": "{count, plural, one {# result} other {# results}}"
  },
  "form": {
    "email_label": "Email Address",
    "required": "This field is required"
  }
}
```

Rules:
- Use descriptive, scoped keys — `form.email_label` not `label1`.
- Keep translation files as the single source of truth for user-facing text.
- Include context comments for translators when the meaning isn't obvious.

### When Hardcoding Is OK

- Brand names (unless they differ by locale)
- Code identifiers shown in developer tools
- Emoji (but be aware of cultural interpretation)
- URLs and technical URIs

---

## Number, Date, and Currency Formatting

### Use the `Intl` API

Never manually format numbers, dates, or currencies. The `Intl` API handles locale-specific conventions.

### Numbers

```ts
// Wrong — manual formatting
function formatNumber(n: number): string {
  return n.toString().replace(/\B(?=(\d{3})+(?!\d))/g, ",");
}
// Returns "1,234.56" — wrong for German (1.234,56) and French (1 234,56)

// Correct — locale-aware
function formatNumber(n: number, locale: string = "en-US"): string {
  return new Intl.NumberFormat(locale).format(n);
}

formatNumber(1234.56, "en-US"); // "1,234.56"
formatNumber(1234.56, "de-DE"); // "1.234,56"
formatNumber(1234.56, "fr-FR"); // "1 234,56"
```

### Currencies

```ts
// Wrong
function formatPrice(amount: number): string {
  return `$${amount.toFixed(2)}`;
}

// Correct
function formatCurrency(amount: number, currency: string, locale: string = "en-US"): string {
  return new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, {
    style: "currency",
    currency,
  }).format(amount);
}

formatCurrency(1234.5, "USD", "en-US"); // "$1,234.50"
formatCurrency(1234.5, "EUR", "de-DE"); // "1.234,50 €"
formatCurrency(1234.5, "JPY", "ja-JP"); // "￥1,235"
```

### Dates

```ts
// Wrong
function formatDate(date: Date): string {
  return `${date.getMonth() + 1}/${date.getDate()}/${date.getFullYear()}`;
}
// MM/DD/YYYY is US-only — most of the world uses DD/MM/YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD

// Correct
function formatDate(date: Date, locale: string = "en-US"): string {
  return new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, { dateStyle: "medium" }).format(date);
}

formatDate(new Date("2026-04-16"), "en-US"); // "Apr 16, 2026"
formatDate(new Date("2026-04-16"), "de-DE"); // "16.04.2026"
formatDate(new Date("2026-04-16"), "ja-JP"); // "2026/04/16"
```

### Relative Time

```ts
function formatRelativeTime(date: Date, locale: string = "en-US"): string {
  const rtf = new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat(locale, { numeric: "auto" });
  const diffMs = date.getTime() - Date.now();
  const diffDays = Math.round(diffMs / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));

  if (Math.abs(diffDays) < 1) return rtf.format(0, "day"); // "today"
  if (Math.abs(diffDays) < 7) return rtf.format(diffDays, "day"); // "3 days ago"
  if (Math.abs(diffDays) < 30) return rtf.format(Math.round(diffDays / 7), "week");
  return rtf.format(Math.round(diffDays / 30), "month");
}
```

### Lists

```ts
// Wrong
function formatList(items: string[]): string {
  return items.join(", ");
}

// Correct — handles "and"/"or" per locale
function formatList(items: string[], locale: string = "en-US", type: "conjunction" | "disjunction" = "conjunction"): string {
  return new Intl.ListFormat(locale, { type }).format(items);
}

formatList(["Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"], "en-US"); // "Alice, Bob, and Charlie"
formatList(["Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"], "de-DE"); // "Alice, Bob und Charlie"
formatList(["Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"], "ja-JP"); // "Alice、Bob、Charlie"
```

---

## Pluralization

Different languages have different plural rules. English has 2 forms (singular, plural). Arabic has 6. Russian has 3.

### ICU Message Format

```ts
// Translation file
{
  "cart_items": "{count, plural, =0 {No items} one {# item} other {# items}} in your cart",
  "notifications": "You have {count, plural, =0 {no notifications} one {# notification} other {# notifications}}"
}

// Usage
t("cart_items", { count: 0 });  // "No items in your cart"
t("cart_items", { count: 1 });  // "1 item in your cart"
t("cart_items", { count: 5 });  // "5 items in your cart"
```

### Don't Concatenate Plurals

```ts
// Wrong — breaks in most languages
const message = `${count} item${count !== 1 ? "s" : ""}`;

// Wrong — word order varies by language
const message = `${t("showing")} ${count} ${t("results")}`;

// Correct — full sentence as one translation key
const message = t("search.results_count", { count });
```

---

## Layout and Text Direction

### RTL (Right-to-Left) Support

If your app may support Arabic, Hebrew, Persian, or Urdu:

```html
<!-- Set direction on html element -->
<html lang="ar" dir="rtl">

<!-- Or per-section -->
<blockquote lang="ar" dir="rtl">
  Arabic quotation here
</blockquote>

<!-- Auto-detect direction for user content -->
<p dir="auto">{userComment}</p>
```

### CSS Logical Properties

Use logical properties instead of physical ones — they automatically flip for RTL.

```css
/* Wrong — doesn't flip for RTL */
.sidebar {
  margin-left: 1rem;
  padding-right: 2rem;
  text-align: left;
  border-left: 2px solid var(--color-border);
}

/* Correct — flips automatically for RTL */
.sidebar {
  margin-inline-start: 1rem;
  padding-inline-end: 2rem;
  text-align: start;
  border-inline-start: 2px solid var(--color-border);
}
```

### Logical Property Mapping

| Physical | Logical | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `margin-left` | `margin-inline-start` | Start of text direction |
| `margin-right` | `margin-inline-end` | End of text direction |
| `padding-left` | `padding-inline-start` | Start of text direction |
| `padding-right` | `padding-inline-end` | End of text direction |
| `text-align: left` | `text-align: start` | Start of text direction |
| `text-align: right` | `text-align: end` | End of text direction |
| `border-left` | `border-inline-start` | Start edge |
| `border-right` | `border-inline-end` | End edge |
| `left` | `inset-inline-start` | Start position |
| `right` | `inset-inline-end` | End position |
| `width` | `inline-size` | Inline axis size |
| `height` | `block-size` | Block axis size |

---

## Text Expansion

Translated text is often longer than English. Design for expansion.

| Language | Typical expansion vs English |
|---|---|
| German | +30% |
| Finnish | +30–40% |
| French | +15–20% |
| Spanish | +20–25% |
| Japanese | Often shorter, but wider characters |
| Arabic | +25% |

### Design Rules

```css
/* Don't set fixed widths on text containers */
.button {
  /* Wrong */
  width: 120px;

  /* Correct — flex to content */
  min-width: fit-content;
  padding-inline: var(--space-4);
}

/* Allow wrapping */
.nav-item {
  white-space: normal; /* Not nowrap */
  overflow-wrap: break-word;
}

/* Use min/max constraints, not fixed */
.card-title {
  max-width: 30ch; /* Character-based, not pixel-based */
}
```

### Testing for Expansion

Use pseudo-localization during development to catch layout issues early:

```ts
// Pseudo-locale: doubles text length and adds accents
// "Save Changes" → "[Šàṽé Ćĥàñĝéš Šàṽé Ćĥàñĝéš]"

function pseudoLocalize(text: string): string {
  const charMap: Record<string, string> = {
    a: "à", b: "ƀ", c: "ć", d: "ð", e: "é", f: "ƒ",
    g: "ĝ", h: "ĥ", i: "ì", j: "ĵ", k: "ķ", l: "ĺ",
    // ...
  };

  const accented = text
    .split("")
    .map((c) => charMap[c.toLowerCase()] ?? c)
    .join("");

  return `[${accented} ${accented}]`; // Double length
}
```

---

## i18n in Framework Context

### React (next-intl / react-intl)

```tsx
import { useTranslations } from "next-intl";

function ProductCard({ product }: { product: Product }) {
  const t = useTranslations("product");

  return (
    <article>
      <h3>{product.name}</h3>
      <p>{t("price", { price: product.price })}</p>
      <p>{t("stock", { count: product.stock })}</p>
    </article>
  );
}
```

### Vue (vue-i18n / @nuxtjs/i18n)

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
const { t, n, d } = useI18n();
</script>

<template>
  <article>
    <h3>{{ product.name }}</h3>
    <p>{{ n(product.price, "currency") }}</p>
    <p>{{ t("product.stock", { count: product.stock }) }}</p>
  </article>
</template>
```

### Framework-Agnostic Rules

- Use the i18n library the project already uses.
- Never build your own i18n system — use established libraries.
- Keep translation keys in a single source (JSON files, CMS, or service).
- Support ICU MessageFormat for pluralization and interpolation.
- Load translations lazily per locale — don't bundle all locales.

---

## Accessibility and i18n

```html
<!-- Always set the lang attribute -->
<html lang="en">

<!-- Mark language changes inline -->
<p>The French word <span lang="fr">bonjour</span> means hello.</p>

<!-- Screen readers use lang to switch pronunciation -->
<blockquote lang="ja">
  こんにちは
</blockquote>
```

---

## Checklist

- [ ] No hardcoded user-facing strings in components
- [ ] Dates use `Intl.DateTimeFormat`, not manual formatting
- [ ] Numbers use `Intl.NumberFormat`, not manual formatting
- [ ] Currencies use `Intl.NumberFormat` with `style: "currency"`
- [ ] Plurals use ICU MessageFormat, not string concatenation
- [ ] Lists use `Intl.ListFormat` for locale-aware joining
- [ ] Layout uses CSS logical properties (`inline-start`, `inline-end`)
- [ ] No fixed widths on text containers — design for 30–40% expansion
- [ ] `lang` attribute is set on `<html>` and on inline language changes
- [ ] `dir` attribute handles RTL when in scope
- [ ] Text is not embedded in images
- [ ] Translations load lazily per locale, not bundled all at once
